title: Drain the Intelligence Community Swamp Act of 2025
short_title: Drain the Intelligence Community Swamp Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill takes away security clearances from 51 former intelligence workers who signed a public letter about Hunter Biden's emails in October 2020. These people will never be allowed to get security clearances again. The bill also requires the Defense Department and Justice Department to investigate what these intelligence workers did.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Armed Services, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Drain the Intelligence Community Swamp Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Revocation of Security Clearances
sections.text: Within twenty four hours after this law is passed, the government must take away security clearances from specific people. A security clearance is special permission to see secret government information. This applies to fifty one former intelligence workers who are listed by name in this section. It also applies to anyone who signed a public letter about Hunter Biden's emails on October nineteenth, twenty twenty. These people can never get their security clearances back or get new ones. The Secretary of Defense and the Attorney General must investigate these people. They must look into what role these people played in the Hunter Biden laptop issue and how much they worked with Biden's presidential campaign. The fifty one people who lose their clearances include former intelligence leaders like Jim Clapper, Mike Hayden, Leon Panetta, and John Brennan, along with many other former intelligence community workers.
